tools.name: check_domain
tools.description:
Check if a domain is available for purchase and get its price.

Always call this before buying. After showing the price, ask the user
two things before proceeding:
1. Confirm they want to purchase at that price.
2. Which payment method they prefer:
   - "card" / "Stripe" → call buy_domain (opens Str

tools.name: check_domains_bulk
tools.description:
Check availability of up to 50 domain names in one call.

Uses fast RDAP lookups (no pricing). Returns a summary with
total/available/taken counts plus per-domain details.

Args:
    domains: List of domain names to check (max 50).

tools.name: suggest_domains
tools.description:
Generate domain name ideas from a keyword and check their availability.

Args:
    keyword: A keyword or short business name (e.g. "taskflow").

tools.name: get_domain_status
tools.description:
Get the status of a domain purchase order.

Polls the backend every 3 seconds (up to 120 seconds) until the order
reaches a terminal state (complete or failed).

Args:
    order_id: The order ID returned from buy_domain (e.g. "ord_abc123").

tools.name: request_transfer_code
tools.description:
Start the transfer verification flow by sending a code to the registrant's email.

Always call this before get_transfer_code or unlock_domain.
Then ask the user to check their email and provide the 6-digit code,
then call verify_transfer_code to get a transfer_token.

Args:
    order_id: The order I

tools.name: verify_transfer_code
tools.description:
Verify the email code and get a transfer token valid for 15 minutes.

Call this after request_transfer_code and the user provides their code.
Pass the returned transfer_token to get_transfer_code or unlock_domain.

Args:
    order_id: The order ID of a completed domain purchase.
    code: The 6-digi

tools.name: get_transfer_code
tools.description:
Get the EPP/transfer authorization code for a completed domain purchase.

Requires a transfer_token from verify_transfer_code.

Args:
    order_id: The order ID of a completed domain purchase.
    transfer_token: Token returned by verify_transfer_code.

tools.name: unlock_domain
tools.description:
Remove the registrar transfer lock. Requires a transfer_token from verify_transfer_code.

Args:
    order_id: The order ID of a completed domain purchase.
    transfer_token: Token returned by verify_transfer_code.

tools.name: renew_domain
tools.description:
Renew a domain for 1 additional year.

Creates a Stripe checkout session for the renewal payment.

Args:
    order_id: The order ID of a completed domain purchase (e.g. "ord_abc123").

tools.name: buy_domain
tools.description:
Start the purchase flow for an available domain via Stripe checkout.

IMPORTANT: Before calling this tool, you MUST first call check_domain
to get the price, then clearly show the user the price and get their
explicit confirmation before proceeding. Never call buy_domain without
the user seeing and

tools.name: buy_domain_crypto
tools.description:
Start the purchase flow for a domain using USDC crypto payment (x402 protocol).

Autonomous two-step payment flow — no browser required:

Step 1: Call this tool to reserve the domain and get a pay_url.
Step 2: Pay the pay_url using your x402 wallet:
  - If you have Coinbase Payments MCP: call its x4

tools.name: buy_domain_mpp
tools.description:
Start the purchase flow for a domain via Stripe's Machine Payments Protocol (MPP).

MPP lets autonomous agents pay with fiat (cards, Link) or stablecoins via
Shared Payment Tokens, with no browser checkout. Two-step flow:

Step 1: Call this tool to get an order_id and pay_url.
Step 2: Make an HTTP G

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "verify_transfer_code" appears informational but accepts parameters (code) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_transfer_code" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the EPP/transfer authorization code for a completed domain purchase. Requires a transfer_token from verify_transfer_code. Args: order_id: The order ID of a …

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "buy_domain" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Start the purchase flow for an available domain via Stripe checkout. IMPORTANT: Before calling this tool, you MUST first call check_domain to get the price, the…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "buy_domain" is 1507 chars, far longer than this server's median (293). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Start the purchase flow for an available domain via Stripe checkout. IMPORTANT: Before calling this tool, you MUST first call check_domain to get the price, the…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "buy_domain" stacks urgency/override words (important, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: request_transfer_code, verify_transfer_code, buy_domain, buy_domain_crypto, buy_domain_mpp · Private data: check_domain, verify_transfer_code, get_transfer_code, unlock_domain, buy_domain, buy_domain_crypto, buy_domain_mpp · Public sink: request_transfer_code, verify_transfer_code, buy_domain, buy_domain_crypto, buy_domain_mpp · Destructive: unlock_domain, buy_domain, buy_domain_crypto, buy_domain_mpp.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: request_transfer_code, verify_transfer_code, buy_domain, buy_domain_crypto, buy_domain_mpp · Private data: check_domain, verify_transfer_code, get_transfer_code, unlock_domain, buy_domain, buy_domain_crypto, buy_domain_mpp · Public sink: request_transfer_code, verify_transfer_code, buy_domain, buy_domain_crypto, buy_domain_mpp · Destructive: unlock_domain, buy_domain, buy_domain_crypto, buy_domain_mpp

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
